name: diagnostic-execution-protocol
description: Work backward from the acceptance condition. Every action is either closing the gap or it isn't. Frame each failure mode as a named fault with a concrete remedy.
---

# Diagnostic Execution Protocol

*Work backward from the acceptance condition. Every action is either closing the gap or it isn't.*

## Root the work in the acceptance condition first

Before any command runs, identify what the task accepts as complete: the exact output path, the exact file format, the exact behavioral postcondition. These are the leaves of your fault tree — the conditions that must be true when the session ends.

Read the instruction completely. Every named path, format, shape, and constraint is a leaf condition. Approximating any of them produces a zero on that condition regardless of how correct the rest is.

## Fault: insufficient environment knowledge

Remedy: open with a parallel batch of reads covering every file the instruction names and every listing that reveals what is present. Do not serialize reads that don't depend on each other. Do not re-read content already in context.

## Fault: discovery consuming turns that should go to building

Remedy: stop discovering when you have enough to determine the shape of the deliverable and the constraints it must satisfy. Additional exploration that doesn't change what you build is a turn cost with no return. Build something. A file on disk is a better base than a complete mental model with nothing written.

## Fault: artifact missing or at wrong path

Remedy: write the minimum-viable artifact early — the right path, the right structure, even if content is incomplete. Existence at the right path is a scoring condition. Refine from there.

## Fault: script fails in a clean environment

Remedy: the script must be fully self-contained. It installs everything it needs, creates every user and directory it requires, writes every configuration file from scratch, sets every permission, and starts every service. It assumes nothing carried over from an interactive session.

When the task requires multiple services to be running: verify each one independently. A service that starts and immediately exits due to a misconfiguration is not running. Check the actual listening state.

## Fault: configuration patch breaks due to host file structure

Remedy: rewrite the complete configuration file instead of patching a fragment. Patching requires knowing the exact structure of the existing file — block boundaries, ordering rules, indentation. Rewriting requires only knowing the desired final state. More lines, fewer structural assumptions.

When the system provides drop-in configuration directories, use them. They inherit scope from the parent automatically and eliminate block-boundary reasoning entirely.

## Fault: permission or ownership error prevents service operation

Remedy: trace the ownership and permission chain from the service process to every resource it touches — directories, files, sockets, hooks. Set ownership and permissions explicitly. Do not assume defaults are correct.

## Fault: reconstruction artifact fails similarity or correctness check

Remedy: read enough of the target to identify its structure before writing any generator. Write a minimum-viable generator early — something that produces the right shape. Then iterate: generate, compare, adjust. Stop at the acceptance criterion. Do not exit with a description; exit with the artifact.

## Fault: encoded or corrupted data not recovered

Remedy: inspect raw bytes before assuming format. Use format-specific recovery tools before writing custom parsers. When data is described as encoded without a key, look for known-good reference points — magic bytes, format headers, repeated patterns at fixed offsets. Derive the transformation, apply it uniformly, emit the result even if partial.

## Fault: binary analysis stalls at recon

Remedy: run identification, string extraction, dynamic tracing, and normal invocation in parallel. These are cheap and usually sufficient. When the path forward is clear, take it immediately. Write the result to the output path. Recognition is not the deliverable.

## Fault: file saved with corrupted content

Remedy: use the file tool for any content containing shell special characters, template variables, or non-ASCII bytes. Heredocs corrupt these silently under quoting pressure.

## Fault: session ends without verification

Remedy: before closing, confirm every leaf condition:
- Output path exists with the exact filename.
- Content is non-empty and structurally valid — read it back, parse it, or compile it.
- For services: the port is open and responding to requests.
- For compiled artifacts: built with the exact command specified, output at the exact path specified.

Fix any failed condition before exiting. The session has no second pass.
